title: Key Account Manager (Sales)
description:
| Global Sourcing | B2B Sales | Product Procurement 



About the Role

You close. You build. You own your book of business and you're proud of it. This isn't a role for someone who waits for leads to land in their lap. As a Key Account Manager at 
, you're the tip of the spear you prospect, you pitch, you negotiate, and you close. You'll be handling high-stakes conversations with procurement teams and business owners who need reliable global sourcing partners, and you'll be the person who turns those conversations into signed contracts. 



Once a deal is done, you don't hand it off and move on, you're the quarterback of the client relationship, connecting them to our product sourcing and logistics teams to make sure delivery on what you promised. This role comes with a competitive base pay plus commissions on every deal you close. The upside is real and so is the accountability. 



What You'll Be Doing



Strategic Business Development: Build your own pipeline. Partner with the Sales & Marketing Manager and Marketing team on targeted lead generation campaigns - but you know how to hunt independently too. Identify, qualify, and pursue new business opportunities across industries that rely on bulk product sourcing. 



Targeted Industry Sourcing: While we can technically source anything from China, you will actively target bulk B2B buyers specifically within high-demand sectors such as healthcare (hospital beds, wheelchairs, PPE), renewable energy (solar equipment and supplies), commercial furniture, security (CCTV), IT hardware, etc.



Enterprise Negotiation & Deal Closing: Lead pricing negotiations for bulk orders, long-term contracts, and recurring procurement deals. Navigate complex procurement cycles with multiple stakeholders without losing 
ntum. Own the process from first contact through to contract signature - you're not handing this off mid-way. Develop and present tailored sourcing proposals and pricing strategies that work for both the client and 
. 



Client Relationship Management: Serve as the primary point of contact for your clients from contract signing onward. Act as the liaison between clients and our product sourcing and logistics teams. Proactively manage expectations, flag delays or issues before they become problems, and always show up with solutions. Build long-term relationships that drive repeat business, upsells, and referrals. 



Quota & Leadership: Hit monthly and quarterly revenue quotas this is non-negotiable. Maintain an accurate and updated pipeline in the CRM at all times. Provide regular forecasting and deal updates to the Sales & Marketing Manager. 



You're a Great Fit If You...

-Have 7-10 years of senior B2B sales experience, ideally in trading, wholesale, procurement, logistics, or a related field.

-Possess a strong, existing network of bulk buyers and key decision-makers in the Philippines across our core target industries: medical and healthcare facilities, renewable energy, commercial real estate/furniture, and IT/security infrastructure.

-Can confidently engage C-suite and senior procurement stakeholders. 

-Know how to negotiate without burning bridges - you're firm, fair, and smart about it. 

-Are comfortable with ambiguity you figure things out, you don't wait to be told. 

-Have strong communication and follow-through. Are self-driven and take full accountability for your pipeline and results. 



Nice to Have

-Direct experience managing high-volume import/export sales from China to the Philippines.

-Existing network and Deep familiarity with local Philippine market dynamics and regulations for importing medical equipment, solar tech, CCTV, and IT hardware.

-Experience closing high-value deals far exceeding the $50K-$500K+ range.

-Familiarity with freight, logistics, and supply chain basics.



What We Offer



-Competitive base salary we respect your time and experience. 

-Commission on every deal you close no cap. 

-A real product to sell: end-to-end global sourcing that genuinely helps businesses cut costs and source smarter. 

-A lean, fast-moving team where your wins are visible and celebrated. 

-Room to grow - as we scale, the people who built the business will be first in line for bigger opportunities.

title: Ninja Marketing Assistance For  Fast Growing Business
description:
About the Role



We're looking for a sharp, proactive Marketing Assistant to support the growth of Digital Brand Builders and our wider portfolio. You'll work directly alongside the founding team, helping execute across paid advertising, funnels, content, and marketing operations. This is a hands-on role — perfect for someone who wants to be in the engine room of a fast-moving digital business, not just scheduling posts.

We're at the cutting edge of AI adoption in how we run and grow our brands. We use AI tools daily across content, research, copywriting, and operations — and we expect this person to be the same way. If you're already experimenting with AI to work faster and smarter, you'll fit right in.



Key Responsibilities



Meta Ads & Paid Traffic



- Assist in setting up, monitoring, and reporting on Meta 
/Instagram) ad campaigns

- Pull performance data from Ads Manager and flag opportunities or issues

- Organise ad creative assets — copy, images, video — for testing cycles

- Help manage audiences, pixel events, and campaign structures



Funnel & Conversion Support



- Assist in building and updating landing pages and funnels (Kartra or similar)

- Monitor key funnel metrics: opt-in rates, cost per lead, conversion rates

- Coordinate funnel assets including forms, thank-you pages, and
 sequences

- Support A/B testing by documenting variants and collating results



Content & Marketing Operations



- Help schedule and publish content across social channels

- Assist in producing marketing materials: ad copy,
 drafts, lead magnets

- Maintain the marketing calendar and ensure deadlines are met

- Keep asset folders, trackers, and dashboards organised and up to date



AI-Assisted Workflows



- Use AI tools (Claude, ChatGPT, and others) to accelerate content creation, research, and reporting

- Help identify and implement AI-driven efficiencies across marketing tasks

- Stay curious about new AI tools and bring ideas for how we can apply them in the business



Reporting & Analysis



- Compile weekly performance reports across ads, funnels, and

- Maintain marketing dashboards (Google Sheets or similar)

- Flag what's working and what needs attention based on the data





What We're Looking For



Basic working knowledge of Meta Ads Manager (campaign setup, reading metrics)

Familiarity with funnel tools — Kartra, ClickFunnels, or equivalent

Comfortable using AI tools in day-to-day work (ChatGPT, Claude, etc.) — we expect AI to be part of your workflow, not an afterthought

Strong attention to detail and ability to manage multiple tasks

A learner's mindset — eager to understand why things work, not just execute tasks

Good written communication skills

Experience with Google Sheets / basic spreadsheet skills





Nice to Have



Experience with
 marketing platforms (ActiveCampaign, Klaviyo, etc.)

Basic understanding of direct response copywriting

Exposure to e-commerce or high-ticket coaching businesses

Experience using AI for content creation, research, or workflow automation

Curiosity about emerging AI tools and how they can be applied to marketing — we're actively building AI into our systems and want someone who gets excited by that, not intimidated





What You'll Get



Direct mentorship from experienced digital entrepreneurs

Exposure to the full lifecycle of a digital marketing business

Fast-paced environment where your work has real, measurable impact

A front-row seat to how a modern, AI-first marketing operation is built

Opportunity to grow into a more senior marketing or operations role

title: Claude Code and AI Automation Expert
description:
About the Role

We're not looking for someone who's read about AI automation. We're looking for someone who's already building with it.

Digital Brand Builders is a fast-moving digital business operating across e-commerce, high-ticket coaching, and marketing. We've gone all-in on AI as an operational layer — and we're looking for an expert to help us build, automate, and scale using Claude Code and the broader AI tooling ecosystem.

This is a ground-floor opportunity to shape how an entrepreneurial organisation uses AI — not maintain legacy systems, but architect new ones.



What You'll Be Building

Claude Code & Agentic Workflows



Design and build agentic workflows using Claude Code for real business tasks — research, content, ops, outreach, and more

Connect Claude to our existing tools and data sources via MCP servers and APIs

Build multi-step AI pipelines that reduce manual work across the business



Automation & Integration



Automate repetitive marketing, sales, and operations tasks using AI-native tooling

Integrate AI workflows with our CRM (Close),
 platforms, funnel tools (Kartra), and Google Workspace

Build internal tools, dashboards, and scripts that make the team faster



AI Systems & Infrastructure



Maintain and improve our growing library of prompts, agents, and automations

Identify high-leverage opportunities where AI can replace or augment manual processes

Document systems so the wider team can use and build on them



Staying Ahead of the Curve



Monitor the latest developments in AI tooling — models, APIs, frameworks — and bring relevant innovations to the business

Test and evaluate new tools before they become mainstream

Be the person in the room who's already used the thing everyone else just heard about





What We're Looking For



Hands-on experience with Claude Code and the Anthropic API

Strong understanding of agentic AI systems, prompt engineering, and multi-step pipelines

Comfortable working with APIs, webhooks, and integration tools (Zapier, Make, or custom)

Ability to write clean, functional code — Python and/or JavaScript preferred

Experience with MCP servers or building custom Claude tool integrations is a strong plus

Self-directed — you don't need hand-holding, you need a problem and a deadline

Entrepreneurial mindset — you think in systems, not tasks





Nice to Have



Experience with other frontier AI tools — OpenAI, Gemini, Perplexity, Cursor, etc.

Background in marketing, e-commerce, or sales operations

Familiarity with CRMs, funnel builders, or
 automation platforms

Experience building internal tools or lightweight SaaS products

Active presence in AI communities — you're learning in public, sharing what you find





What You'll Get



Work directly with founders who are actively building at the frontier of AI adoption

Real creative and technical autonomy — we won't slow you down with committees

A business with genuine use cases across marketing, sales, ops, and e-commerce — plenty to build

Competitive compensation with room to grow as the role grows

The satisfaction of building things that actually get used





If your idea of a good day is shipping an AI workflow that saves the team hours every week — we want to hear from you.

title: Bookkeepeer & Trust Accountant
description:
Blambles Finance Group – Bookkeeper (Xero & Property Trust Accounting)



Who we are

We are a Brisbane-based boutique firm active since 2017, spanning commercial property buyers agency, commercial property finance broking, and residential mortgage broking. We are expanding into general insurance broking and commercial property management. Our mission is to help individuals and businesses build wealth via property. This role supports growth and works closely with our Filipino-based virtual assistants (currently 4) and Australian staff (currently 3).



Three core functions

- Bookkeeping (Xero): bank reconciliations, AP/AR, expense coding (utilising HubDoc), payroll, BAS support, and client ledgers

- Management accounting: financial reporting, variance analysis, budgeting support, and performance reporting

- Property trust accounting: recording/reconciling trust receipts and disbursements, trust account records, regulatory compliance for trust funds, onboarding of clients/suppliers



Key requirements

- Xero bookkeeping experience (practice and/or client bookkeeping), including reconciliations, AP/AR, BAS support, and payroll

- Management accounting and reporting experience

- Property management trust accounting knowledge (trust receipts/disbursements, trust banking, statutory compliance)

- Highly proficient in Microsoft Office 365, with excellent Excel skills

- Excellent English communication (written and oral)

- Self-motivated, detail-oriented, and able to prioritise competing tasks/deadlines

- Stable employment history

- Ability to work independently and with a remote team



You will be rewarded with

- 100% remote role (work from home)

- Competitive remuneration, discretionary bonus, and public holiday/annual leave

- Flexible working environment with Australian-based hours (AEST)

- Collaboration with Filipino support contractors and Australian staff

- Opportunity to influence Xero bookkeeping and property management processes and systems



Key responsibilities

- Bookkeeping (Xero):

  - Bank reconciliations, accounts payable/receivable, expense coding (HubDoc)

  - BAS preparation support and reporting

  - Maintaining accurate client ledgers and financial records

  - Payroll for Australian-based staff and contractors in the Philippines

- Management accounting:

  - Prepare and support financial reporting and analysis

  - Assist with budgeting and performance reporting

- Property trust accounting:

  - Record and reconcile trust receipts and disbursements

  - Maintain trust account records in line with relevant regulations

  - Onboard new clients and suppliers

  - Ensure exemplary customer service to stakeholders

- Compliance:

  - Adhere to internal/external policies, procedures, and applicable laws (including Privacy Act 1988 and Australian Privacy Principles)

- Other duties:

  - Ad hoc tasks and projects as required

  - Complete tasks accurately and on time

  - Training and career progression opportunities



Qualifications and selection criteria (minimum)

- Relevant accounting qualifications

- Proven Xero bookkeeping experience (reconciliations, AP/AR, BAS support)

- Familiarity with property management (commercial preferred) and trust accounting

- Strong Microsoft Office 365 skills, especially Excel

- Excellent written and verbal English

- Self-motivated, proactive, and able to manage multiple tasks and deadlines

- Clean disciplinary history and stable employment history

- Willingness to coordinate with our Filipino-based team



To apply

Please
 
 with the subject: BFG job application – Bookkeeper & Trust Accountant

Enclose your resume and a cover letter addressing:

- Availability to start

- Evidence of current internet speed

- Evidence of current computer hardware/system specs

- Willingness to supply your own internet, laptop, and headset

- Why you want this role

- Why you are a good fit based on past experience

- Current remuneration and expected remuneration



Online assessment

Please complete the short online test: 
/s/x9adrflu



Notes

- Only shortlisted applicants who meet minimum criteria and score well in the online test will be contacted.

- You will typically hear back within 7 days if shortlisted; otherwise, your application has been unsuccessful.

- Do not apply via OnlineJobs

title: Remote Customer Service Representative (US Hours)
description:
We are looking for motivated, reliable, and professional individuals to join our growing remote team as Customer Service Representatives. This is a great opportunity to work with a US-based company from the comfort of your own home.



About the Role



As a Customer Service Representative, you will be the front line of communication between our company and customers or clients. You will handle outbound and/or inbound calls, assist with inquiries, gather information, and ensure every interaction is handled with professionalism and care. Specific assignments and projects may vary based on business needs — successful agents may be placed on different accounts and campaigns as they become available.



Key Responsibilities



Handle outbound and/or inbound calls in a professional and courteous manner

Communicate clearly and effectively with English-speaking clients and customers

Follow call scripts, workflows, and company guidelines

Accurately document call outcomes and information

Meet performance benchmarks and maintain a good attendance record

Adapt to different account types and campaign requirements as assigned



Schedule



Days and hours will vary depending on the client and account assigned

All shifts are aligned to US time zones (Eastern, Central, Mountain, or Pacific)

Primarily covering afternoon to overnight Philippine time

Flexibility and the ability to adjust to different schedules is a plus

Training & Onboarding



Paid training provided prior to going live

Full support during onboarding to set you up for success



Probation Period



First 30 days is a probationary period

Salary increases available based on attendance and performance after probation



Requirements



Fluent, clear spoken and written English — this is non-negotiable

Reliable, stable internet connection

Computer running Windows 11 Pro

Noise-cancelling headset

Quiet, dedicated workspace suitable for professional calls

Ability to commit to a consistent US-hour night shift schedule

Positive attitude and strong work ethic



Compensation



Starting salary: $26,500 monthly, paid bi-weekly

Salary increase opportunities after the 30-day probation based on productivity and attendance

Competitive pay with room to grow as more accounts become available



How to Apply

To be considered, please submit a voice recording introducing yourself and demonstrating your English communication skills. Applications WITHOUT a voice note will NOT be considered.

Apply now and be first in line when new accounts open up!

title: Remote Federal Contract Research & Proposal Specialist
description:
About Us



US Veterans Solutions is a U.S.-based, Service-Disabled Veteran-Owned Small Business (SDVOSB) focused on securing and executing federal government contracts.



We are actively building a high-performance remote support team to assist with identifying, organizing, and preparing competitive proposals for federal opportunities.



This is an opportunity to work directly with leadership on real contract pursuits—not general VA work.



Position Overview



We are seeking a highly detail-oriented, reliable, and intelligent professional to support our federal contracting efforts on a project-by-project basis.



This is NOT a salaried role. Work is assigned based on active contract opportunities. Ideal for someone who prefers flexible, performance-based work and wants to grow within U.S. government contracting.



What You Will Be Doing



You will play a critical role in helping us identify and prepare for contract opportunities:



Research federal contract opportunities (primarily via SAM.gov and related platforms)

Review solicitations and extract key requirements

Organize documents and build structured proposal folders

Assist in drafting and formatting proposal sections (based on templates and guidance)

Assemble complete proposal packages for final executive review

Track deadlines, submission requirements, and compliance items

Who This Role Is For



This role is ideal for someone who:



Is highly organized and detail-driven

Can read and understand complex documents

Takes ownership and follows instructions precisely

Communicates clearly and professionally in English

Wants to build long-term experience in U.S. federal contracting

Is motivated by growth, performance, and opportunity

Preferred Experience (Strong Advantage)

Administrative support, research, or document management

Experience with procurement, bidding, or proposal preparation

Familiarity with U.S. government contracting platforms (SAM.gov, etc.)

Experience working with structured processes or compliance-heavy work

Bonus / Highly Preferred Skills



Candidates with the following skillsets will stand out:



Experience using AI tools (ChatGPT, Claude, etc.) for research, drafting, and workflow efficiency

Strong writing and editing skills (professional, structured communication)

Experience with RFP/RFQ response development

Familiarity with Microsoft Word formatting (styles, tables, compliance formatting)

Experience with Excel or Google Sheets for tracking opportunities

CRM or project management tools (Trello, Asana, ClickUp, etc.)

Basic understanding of U.S. business, contracting, or compliance processes

Ability to analyze documents and summarize key requirements quickly

Experience supporting U.S.-based clients or companies

Growth Opportunity (IMPORTANT)



This role is designed to grow with the company.



For the right individual who demonstrates reliability, accuracy, and strong performance, there is a clear path toward:



Consistent, ongoing work

Increased responsibility and leadership in proposal development

The opportunity to transition into a full-time role



Target compensation of up to $20/hour at 40 hours per week may be attainable within 12 months for high-performing individuals who grow with the company and contribute to successful contract execution.



Work Structure

100% Remote

Project-based (no fixed monthly hours initially)

Flexible schedule, but deadlines are strict

Work volume will increase as contracts increase

Compensation

Paid per project/task (not hourly salary at start)

Rates based on complexity and experience

Performance-based growth opportunities

Potential for long-term, stable work as contract pipeline expands

Important Notes

This is a performance-based opportunity, not a traditional salaried position at the start

You will be working directly with a U.S.-based CEO

Company is transitioning branding from US Veterans Security to US Veterans Solutions

Application Instructions (IMPORTANT)



To be considered, you MUST follow these steps:



Submit your resume

Include a short paragraph explaining your experience with research, admin work, or proposals

Answer this question:

“How would you approach reviewing a contract document to identify key requirements?”

Include the word PRECISION at the top of your application



Applications that do not follow instructions will not be considered.



What We Are Building



We are building a long-term, high-performance remote team to support federal contract operations.



This is an opportunity to be part of something that is growing, structured, and results-driven.



We are not looking for average. We are looking for professionals who execute with precision and are ready to grow.

title: SEO EXECUTION VA (SearchAtlas-Based) – REAL ESTATE
description:
ROLE OVERVIEW

I'm hiring an SEO Execution Virtual Assistant, not a strategist and not an agency.

This role is for someone who already understands on-page SEO and page-level optimization and can execute tasks inside a defined system using SearchAtlas.

You will not be "learning SEO from scratch."

You will be trained on my workflows and tools, but you must already understand how SEO actually works.

This role supports a U.S.-based real estate business focused on local SEO and geographic market domination.



WHAT YOU WILL DO (NON-NEGOTIABLE)

• Perform keyword research inside SearchAtlas

• Map one keyword ? one page correctly

• Analyze SERPs and identify intent mismatches

• Optimize existing blog posts and landing pages

• Create SEO-optimized blog drafts

• Write meta titles and descriptions

• Implement internal links intentionally

• Apply page-level schema (basic)

• Monitor Google Search Console for indexing and issues

• Track rankings and maintain a simple SEO log

• Execute video-to-blog SEO workflows

• Explain your work during live screen-share reviews

If you don't know how to do any of the above without guessing, this role is not a fit.



REQUIRED EXPERIENCE (PLEASE READ CAREFULLY)

You must:

• Understand the difference between a keyword and a page

• Understand search intent (informational vs local vs transactional)

• Have optimized at least one real blog or page before

• Have used any SEO tool (Ahrefs, SEMrush, Surfer, etc.)

• Be comfortable working inside a CMS or website builder

• Be willing to do live screen-share audits

• Be able to explain why you made SEO changes

If you have never optimized a page that ranks, do not apply.



TOOLS YOU WILL USE

• SearchAtlas (training on this tool provided)

• Google Search Console

• Google Analytics (basic)

• GoHighLevel (GHL)

• Google Docs / Sheets



PAY & STRUCTURE

• 10–15 hours per week

• Long-term role

• Paid trial week (reduced rate)

• Weekly reviews and feedback

• Clear SOPs and checklists

Performance determines pay increases.



HOW TO APPLY (REQUIRED)

Your application must answer these questions:

1. What is the difference between a keyword and a page?

2. What does search intent mean?

3. What SEO tools have you used?

4. Have you optimized a blog post before? Briefly explain what you did.

5. Are you comfortable doing a live screen-share test?

Applications without answers will be ignored.



This role includes a live screen-share test. If that makes you uncomfortable, do not apply.

title: Senior UX Designer
description:
Senior UX Designer

Part-Time Contract | ~39 weeks | Remote (Philippines)



-------

About Us



2 Dam Creative is an Australian digital agency with offices in Australia, the Philippines and the UK. We deliver large-scale web and mobile platforms for enterprise clients across government, health, membership organisations, and not-for-profit sectors. We are expanding our design team and looking for an experienced Senior UX Designer to join us on a specific project engagement.



----------

About the Role



This is a part-time, work-from-home contract running approximately 39 weeks. The total commitment is around 390 hours (roughly 49 working days) spread across the project lifecycle, with the workload varying by phase. You will be embedded within our Australian delivery team, working on a complex, multi-platform project that spans a responsive national website, a branded member mobile app, CRM integrations, and multiple third-party system touchpoints.



----------

Indicative Weekly Commitment



The heavier weeks are front-loaded. During the build phase, the role shifts to accessibility testing, design QA, and occasional UAT support.



39 weeks | ~390 hours / ~49 days



------------

What You Will Be Doing



 > Planning and conducting user research using qualitative and quantitative methods, then translating findings into clear, actionable recommendations

> Creating user personas, journey maps, information architecture, and interaction models that guide product strategy and design direction

> Designing intuitive, accessible interfaces for both web and mobile, with a strong focus on WCAG 2.1 AA+ compliance

> Building interactive prototypes and wireframes in Figma to communicate design intent to developers and stakeholders

> Producing developer-ready specifications, design systems, and component libraries that the build team can work from directly

> Collaborating with art directors, content specialists, developers, and project managers across Australian and international time zones

> Supporting accessibility and usability testing throughout the build and QA phases

> Mentoring junior designers and contributing to the continuous improvement of our UX processes and standards



------

What We Are Looking For



> At least five years of hands-on experience in UX research and design for digital products, ideally across both web and native mobile

> A degree in Human-Computer Interaction, Psychology, Design, or a related field (or equivalent professional experience)

> Strong working knowledge of WCAG accessibility standards and inclusive design principles

> Expert-level proficiency in Figma, including design systems, prototyping, auto-layout, and developer handoff workflows

> Experience working within complex integration environments where UX decisions need to account for CRM data, tiered access models, SSO, or payment flows

> Solid analytical skills with the ability to synthesise research data into recommendations that non-technical stakeholders can act on

> Confident communication and presentation skills, comfortable working with senior client stakeholders

> Self-motivated, reliable, and able to work independently in a remote environment while staying closely connected to the wider team

> A portfolio that demonstrates your end-to-end UX process, not just final visual outputs



-------

Nice to Have



> Experience with AI-assisted design tools such as Figma AI

> Familiarity with analytics and session recording tools (GA4, Hotjar, Mixpanel, or similar)

> Experience with content-heavy platforms requiring complex information architecture

> Previous work with Australian clients or within Australian digital agencies



--------

What We Offer



> Day shift hours: 8:00 AM to 4:00 PM Philippine time (on scheduled working days)

> Part-time contract, work from home

> Competitive day rate

> The opportunity to work on a genuinely interesting, large-scale project with a senior, collaborative team



-------

How to Apply



Submit your CV, portfolio, and a brief cover note explaining why this role is the right fit for you.



If you are shortlisted following the initial interview, the next stage will include a practical skills assessment to help us find the best match for the role.

Shopify Growth

Optimize product pages (images, copy, offers, bundles)

Identify and fix drop-offs and friction points

Amazon Growth

Audit and improve listings (titles, bullets, images, A+ content)

Monitor performance, reviews, and account health

Identify ways to increase sales and reduce returns

Improve listing quality and customer experience

Brand & Product Strategy Implementation

Ensure consistent positioning, messaging, and premium presentation

Customer Experience & Operations

Influencer/Outreach VA

Content & Growth Support

Reporting & Continuous Improvement

what was done

results

insights

next actions

Requirements

4+ years experience in Shopify ecommerce

title: Go High Level funnel and automation expert
description:
About the Project

I run an IELTS test preparation brand (IELTS Ebook Empire) selling digital ebooks, mock test bundles, and paid 1:1 coaching sessions to IELTS candidates targeting Band 7+. I need an experienced GoHighLevel specialist to build a complete, multi-branch quiz funnel system natively inside GHL — including all funnel pages, surveys, two-step order forms, workflow automations,
 sequence triggers, calendar booking, and Stripe payment integration.

A comprehensive build specification document has already been written. It contains the full funnel architecture, every page’s copy, design direction, element-by-element layout specs, tag architecture, workflow logic, and GHL AI Build prompts. Your job is to execute this spec inside GoHighLevel, not to write copy or design from scratch. The thinking is done — I need a builder who can turn a detailed blueprint into a live, fully functioning funnel system.



What You Will Build Inside GoHighLevel

The system is a quiz-to-sale funnel that captures IELTS candidates via a single quiz entry point, routes them to one of four skill-specific branches (Writing, Reading, Speaking, Listening), and guides each lead through a price ladder: free lead magnet, $9 tripwire ebook, $37–$47 mock test upsell bundle, and paid 1:1 coaching session. Everything lives natively inside GHL.

Asset Inventory — What Gets Built

GHL Asset

Qty

Description

1 Quiz Landing Page

1 Standalone GHL funnel page with full-screen navy hero section, social proof strip, and CTA. Hosts the embedded GHL Survey.

1 GHL Survey

4  2-slide survey: Slide 1 is a single-choice routing question (4 answer options). Slide 2 captures first name and
. Conditional logic redirects each answer to its branch funnel.

4  Branch Lead Magnet Funnels. One per IELTS skill. Each has 4–5 steps: confirmation + diagnosis, problem naming, tripwire offer introduction, offer card with GHL two-step order form, and delivery reminder page.

4. Branch Upsell Funnels One per skill. Each has 3 steps: celebration bridge, upsell offer card with GHL two-step order form, and purchase confirmation.

4. Branch Coaching Funnels. One per skill. Each has 4 steps: authority hero, session details, branch-specific focus copy, and calendar booking with price display.

8 GHL Two-Step Order Forms. One on each tripwire offer page ($9) and one on each upsell page ($37–$47). Connected to Stripe.

1. GHL Calendar Coaching session calendar with 45-minute slots, auto-timezone detection, embedded on all 4 coaching funnel Step 4 pages.

5–8 GHL Workflows. Survey submit workflow (4-branch If/Else), tripwire purchase workflow, upsell purchase workflow, coaching booking workflow, and cold lead re-engagement workflow.

17 GHL Tags. Pre-defined tag set for lead segmentation, buyer tracking, and automation triggers across all branches.

6 GHL Custom Fields. Contact fields for branch tracking, UTM parameters, funnel source, and product purchase history.

GHL Pipeline

2 IELTS Leads pipeline (4 stages) and Coaching pipeline (3 stages).

8 Stripe Products 4x $9 tripwire products + 4x $37–$47 upsell products created in Stripe and linked to GHL order forms.

1 Meta Pixel Integration

Base pixel code installed across all funnels with event-specific triggers (Lead, ViewContent, InitiateCheckout, Purchase, Schedule).



Total Funnel Pages

Quiz landing page: 1 page

Lead magnet funnels: 4 branches × 5 steps = 20 pages

Upsell funnels: 4 branches × 3 steps = 12 pages

Coaching funnels: 4 branches × 4 steps = 16 pages



Detailed Scope of Work

Below is a step-by-step breakdown of everything included in this engagement. Each task references a specific section of the build spec document you will receive.

Phase 1: GHL Account Setup & Foundation

Before building any pages, you will configure the backend infrastructure that powers the entire system.

Create 17 GHL tags across four categories: lead tags (4), tripwire buyer tags (4), core upsell buyer tags (4), coaching booked tags (4), and one cold-lead re-engagement tag. Exact tag names are defined in the spec.

Create 6 custom contact fields for branch tracking (ielts_branch), funnel source attribution (funnel_source), UTM passthrough (utm_source, utm_campaign), and product purchase tracking (tripwire_product, core_product).

Create 2 GHL pipelines: IELTS Leads (stages: New Lead ? Tripwire Buyer ? Core Buyer ? Coaching Booked) and Coaching (stages: Booked ? Pre-Session ? Completed).

Connect Stripe to the GHL account. Create 8 products in Stripe (4 tripwire products at $9 each, 4 upsell products at $37–$47 each) and link them to GHL.

Set up GHL Calendar for coaching sessions: 45-minute appointment slots, auto-timezone detection, and booking confirmation
.

Install Google Fonts via custom CSS in each funnel’s header code (Playfair Display for headlines, Inter for body, Source Code Pro for labels). CSS import snippet is provided in the spec.

Phase 2: Shared Quiz Entry Point

The entry funnel is the single page all paid ad traffic lands on.

Build the quiz landing page as a GHL funnel with one step. Full-screen navy hero section with eyebrow label, three-line headline, sub-headline, social proof strip, teal CTA button, and trust micro-copy. All copy is provided word-for-word in the spec.

Create the GHL Survey with two slides. Slide 1: single-choice routing question with four card-style answer options (Writing, Reading, Speaking, Listening), each with a sub-label. Auto-advance on selection. Slide 2: first name and
 capture with personalised headline and teal submit button.

Configure survey conditional logic: four redirect rules that route each answer to its branch-specific result funnel URL.

Embed the survey on the landing page below the hero section.

Install Meta Pixel base code on the funnel and fire the Lead event on survey submission.

Phase 3: Branch Lead Magnet Funnels (Build 1, Clone 3)

Build the Writing branch first as the template funnel, then clone and adapt for Reading, Speaking, and Listening.

Build the Writing Lead Magnet funnel with 4–5 steps. Step 1: navy confirmation screen with success badge, headline, body copy, pull quote (gold left border), and scroll CTA. Step 2: cream problem-naming section with diagnostic headline, body, 3-stat strip, and system description. Step 3: navy tripwire introduction with gold eyebrow, offer bridge copy, and red urgency line. Step 4: cream offer card with product title, $9/$27 price display, 6-item teal-tick checklist, FOMO notice, GHL two-step order form, trust line, and skip link. Step 5 (skip destination): teal-pale delivery reminder.

Add confetti script to Step 1 using the JavaScript snippet provided in the spec (canvas-confetti library).

Configure the GHL two-step order form on Step 4: connect to the $9 Writing Stripe product. Pre-fill contact info from the survey submission. On successful payment, redirect to the Writing upsell funnel.

Clone the Writing funnel three times. Rename each clone to Reading, Speaking, and Listening. Swap all copy, product names, checklist items, stats, and pull quotes using the branch-specific content provided in the spec document. Update each order form to point to the correct Stripe product.

Phase 4: Branch Upsell Funnels (Build 1, Clone 3)

Each upsell funnel loads immediately after a $9 tripwire purchase.

Build the Writing Upsell funnel with 3 steps. Step 1: navy celebration hero with gold badge, headline, italic teal sub-headline, and bridge copy. Step 2: cream offer card with product title ($47 Mock Test + Examiner Feedback Bundle), $47/$97 price display, 6-item checklist, urgency box, GHL two-step order form, and skip link. Step 3: teal-pale purchase confirmation with delivery instructions and 3-step next actions.

Configure the GHL two-step order form on Step 2: connect to the $47 Writing Stripe product.

Clone three times for Reading ($37), Speaking ($47), and Listening ($37). Swap all copy and reconnect order forms to correct Stripe products.

Phase 5: Branch Coaching Appointment Funnels (Build 1, Clone 3)

Each coaching funnel is a premium, paid 1:1 session booking flow.

Build the Writing Coaching funnel with 4 steps. Step 1: navy authority hero with gold eyebrow, headline, PhD credentials body, 3-item credential strip, and CTA. Step 2: cream session details with 3 numbered cards (Before/During/After), format info, and CTA. Step 3: cream branch-specific focus with Writing-specific headline and body copy. Step 4: navy calendar and pricing section with gold price display ($97), embedded GHL Calendar widget, preparation instructions, teal-pale reassurance box, and confirm button.

Embed the GHL Calendar on Step 4 and configure timezone auto-detection.

Clone three times for Reading, Speaking, and Listening. Only Step 3 copy changes between branches — the rest is identical.

Phase 6: GHL Workflow Automations

Build the workflow layer that connects every funnel action to the correct tag, sequence, and pipeline movement.

Workflow 1 — Survey Submit: Triggered when the IELTS Quiz Survey is submitted. Uses If/Else branching to check the ielts_branch custom field. For each branch: applies the correct lead tag, adds the contact to the IELTS Leads pipeline (New Lead stage), and starts Sequence A for that branch.

Workflow 2 — Tripwire Purchase: Triggered on $9 order form payment. Applies the correct tripwire-buyer tag, sets the tripwire_product custom field, redirects the buyer to their branch upsell funnel, and starts Sequence B.

Workflow 3 — Upsell Purchase: Triggered on $37–$47 order form payment. Applies the correct core-buyer tag, sets the core_product custom field, moves the contact to Core Buyer pipeline stage, and starts Sequence C.

Workflow 4 — Coaching Booking: Triggered on GHL Calendar booking confirmation. Applies the coaching-booked tag, moves to the Coaching pipeline (Booked stage), sends a confirmation
 with pre-session instructions, and sends an internal notification.

Workflow 5 — Cold Lead Re-engagement: Triggered when a contact has no
 opens for 30 days. Adds the cold-30d tag. Starts a 3
 re-engagement mini-sequence.

Phase 7: Meta Pixel Events

Configure event-specific pixel fires at each conversion point across all funnels.

Event

Trigger Point

Lead

Survey submission (all branches)

ViewContent

Upsell offer card visible

InitiateCheckout

CTA click on any order form

Purchase ($9)

Tripwire order form payment confirmed

Purchase ($37–$47)

Upsell order form payment confirmed

Schedule

Calendar booking confirmed



Phase 8: Testing & Launch

End-to-end testing: Complete a full test transaction through all 4 branches — quiz ? result page ? $9 purchase ? upsell ? coaching booking. Verify each workflow fires correctly, tags are applied, pipeline stages update, and payment processes through Stripe.

Mobile responsiveness: All 49 funnel pages must render correctly on mobile (iPhone and Android). This is a mobile-first funnel — 80%+ of traffic will come from phones via Instagra
ta ads.

Survey redirect testing: Confirm all 4 conditional redirects route to the correct branch funnel.

Order form testing: Process test Stripe payments on all 8 order forms and verify post-purchase redirects.

Domain connection: Publish all funnels and connect to a custom domain (I will provide the domain and DNS access).



What I Will Provide

You will not be working from vague briefs. Everything is pre-built.

Complete build specification document (40+ pages): Full funnel architecture map, every page’s copy (word-for-word), element-by-element layout specs, colour palette with hex codes, typography direction, tag and custom field definitions, workflow logic, GHL AI Build prompts, Meta Pixel event map, and a phased build checklist.

GHL account access: Full admin access to the GHL sub-account where all assets will be built.

Stripe account access: Connected Stripe account for product creation and payment testing.

Brand assets: Logo files, any required imagery, and brand colour palette.

Domain and DNS access: For connecting published funnels to the live domain.

GHL AI Build prompts: Five ready-to-paste prompts for GHL’s Funnels AI feature to scaffold each page type. These accelerate initial page creation — you then refine, add order forms, embed surveys and calendars, and wire up workflows.

Ongoing availability: I will be available for questions, clarifications, and approvals throughout the build. Response time within 12 hours.

Required Skills & Experience

This is a hands-on GHL build engagement. I need someone who has built and shipped funnel systems inside GoHighLevel before — not someone learning on the job.

Must-Have

Proven GoHighLevel experience: You have built multi-step funnels, surveys, workflows, and order forms inside GHL on at least 3 previous projects. You can show examples or screenshots.

GHL Funnel Builder proficiency: You are comfortable with GHL’s section/row/column page builder, know how to add custom CSS, embed surveys, and configure two-step order forms.

GHL Survey & Conditional Logic: You have configured GHL surveys with conditional redirect rules and branching logic.

GHL Workflow Automation: You can build If/Else branching workflows with tag triggers, custom field updates, pipeline stage movements, and internal notifications.

Stripe integration: You have connected Stripe to GHL, created products, and configured two-step order forms with live payment processing.

Mobile-responsive design: You build mobile-first and test across devices. You understand that GHL’s desktop builder requires manual mobile adjustments.

Meta Pixel setup: You can install a Meta Pixel and fire event-specific triggers (Lead, Purchase, Schedule) at the correct funnel touchpoints.

Nice-to-Have

Experience with GHL’s Funnels & Websites AI (Beta) — the spec includes AI Build prompts that can accelerate scaffolding.

GHL Calendar configuration experience (appointment types, timezone detection, booking confirmation
s).

Familiarity with quiz funnel architecture (e.g., 
, ScoreApp, or similar quiz-to-offer flows).

Experience working from detailed specification documents rather than vague creative briefs.

Understanding of direct-response copywriting principles (not required to write copy, but helps with layout decisions).

Deliverables & Acceptance Criteria

The project is complete when all of the following are delivered and tested inside the GHL account:

1 quiz landing page with embedded GHL Survey, live and rendering correctly on mobile.

1 GHL Survey with 2 slides, 4 conditional redirect rules, and correct custom field mapping.

4 lead magnet funnels (4–5 steps each) with all copy implemented, all design tokens applied, and confetti scripts active on confirmation pages.

4 upsell funnels (3 steps each) with all copy, pricing, and checklists implemented.

4 coaching appointment funnels (4 steps each) with GHL Calendar embedded and branch-specific copy.

8 GHL two-step order forms connected to Stripe, processing test payments successfully.

5–8 GHL workflows with correct tag application, custom field updates, pipeline movements, and sequence triggers.

17 tags and 6 custom fields created and actively used by workflows.

2 GHL pipelines with correct stages.

Meta Pixel installed with event-specific triggers firing at all 6 defined touchpoints.

All funnels published and connected to the custom domain.

A full end-to-end test walk-through recorded on Loom (or equivalent) showing all 4 branch journeys working from quiz to coaching booking.

Timeline & Milestones

Estimated total duration: 10–14 working days. Milestones below are for progress check-ins and phased approval.

Milestone

Working Days

Deliverable for Review

M1: Foundation

Days 1–2

All tags, custom fields, pipelines, Stripe products, and calendar created. Google Fonts CSS installed. Confirmation screenshot.

M2: Quiz Entry

Days 2–3

Quiz landing page live. Survey created with conditional logic. Workflow firing correctly on submit.

M3: Writing Branch

Days 3–5

Full Writing flow
 lead magnet funnel (5 steps), upsell funnel (3 steps), coaching funnel (4 steps). Order forms processing test payments. Workflows triggering.

M4: Remaining Branches

Days 5–9

Reading, Speaking, and Listening branches cloned, copy swapped, order forms reconnected, and tested.

M5: Workflows & Pixel

Days 9–11

All workflows built and tested. Meta Pixel installed with all events. Pipeline movements verified.

M6: Testing & Launch

Days 11–14

Full end-to-end testing on all 4 branches. Mobile responsiveness confirmed. Funnels published on custom domain. Loom walk-through recorded.







TOTAL: 49 funnel pages + 1 survey + 8 order forms + 5–8 workflows



How to Apply

To be considered for this project, please include the following in your proposal:

A brief summary of your GoHighLevel experience — how many GHL funnel projects you have completed, and the types of funnels you have built (quiz funnels, webinar funnels, appointment funnels, etc.).

At least one example of a GHL funnel you have built (screenshot, URL, or Loom walk-through). Multi-step funnels with surveys, order forms, and workflow automations are most relevant.

Confirmation that you have experience with GHL Surveys + conditional logic, GHL two-step order forms + Stripe, and GHL Workflow automations.

Your estimated total cost and preferred payment structure (fixed price, milestone-based, or hourly).

Your availability to start and estimated completion timeline.



Important: This is not a design-from-scratch or copywriting project. Every word of copy, every design direction, every tag name, and every workflow rule is already defined in a detailed specification document. I need a skilled GoHighLevel builder who can execute a blueprint precisely and efficiently. If you have strong GHL skills and enjoy working from detailed specs, this is a great fit.

title: Senior Media Buyer & Creative Strategist
description:
Ad Spend€200,000+ per month

Markets Australia, UK (Denmark: nice-to-have)

Reporting to the founder

Manages Junior Media Buyer(s)

Weight - high media buying role, less creative strategy



About the Role

We're looking for a data-driven, creatively sharp Senior Media Buyer who can own paid media performance across three international markets. You'll sit at the intersection of performance marketing, creative strategy, and team leadership — driving campaigns for health & beauty and/or dropshipping brands from testing through to aggressive scale.

You'll be the go-to authority on what to launch, what to kill, and what to double down on. You'll guide the junior media buyer on execution while you focus on strategy, creative feedback loops, and hitting aggressive ROAS targets.



Key Responsibilities

Campaign Management

Own and manage all paid media campaigns across Meta (primary) and other relevant platforms

Maintain and optimise a €200K+ monthly ad spend with full accountability on performance metrics

Determine campaign architecture: when to use CBO vs ABO, and how to structure cost cap strategies

Direct the junior media buyer on what to launch, pause, or scale — acting as the strategic decision-maker



Testing & Scaling

Design and execute structured creative testing frameworks: different landers, angles, hooks, and formats

Lead product scaling tests — identifying winning products and scaling spend systematically

Kill underperforming ad sets and creatives quickly and decisively based on clear KPIs

Build and manage a performance feedback loop between creative results and production priorities



Creative Strategy

Communicate creative briefs and performance insights clearly to the editors and content production team

Translate data into actionable creative direction — identifying what's working and why

Collaborate with the product research team to align ad strategy with offer and market fit



Cross-Market Oversight

Run and localise campaigns across the Australian and UK markets

Apply market-specific insights around audience behaviour, compliance, and buying patterns

Experience with the Danish market is a strong plus





What You Bring

Must-Have Experience

Proven track record managing €150K–€200K+ per month in paid media spend

Deep expertise in Meta Ads: cost caps, CBOs, ABOs, creative testing at scale

Background in dropshipping and/or health & beauty brands — this is non-negotiable

Experience running campaigns in Australia and/or the UK market

Demonstrated ability to manage and mentor a junior media buyer



Skills & Mindset

Strong analytical mind — comfortable in spreadsheets, attribution tools, and ad managers simultaneously

Decisive and fast: you know when to kill an ad set and you don't hesitate

Clear communicator — able to brief editors and researchers without losing detail or tone

Creative intuition backed by performance data, not just gut feel

Self-sufficient and proactive — you don't wait to be told what to test next



Nice to Have

Familiarity with the Danish market and its consumer behaviour

Experience with advertorial-style funnels and native-to-paid bridge strategies

Knowledge of compliance nuances across AUS, UK, and EU markets for health products





What We Offer

Ownership of strategy across three markets with real budget to execute

A lean, performance-focused team without bureaucratic bottlenecks

Competitive base salary + performance bonus tied to ROAS and spend efficiency

Remote-first setup with flexible working hours

Direct access to leadership and fast decision-making



Interested?

1. Send a 60-second video with your motivation

2. Explain your experience & knowledge in maximum 2 alinea's

3. Send an
 with the subject - MEDIA BUYER OG

4. Send the
 to

title: N8N Automation Freelancer (Remote, Flexible)
description:
Hey! I run a bunch of YouTube channels. And we constantly need some tools to let us make content faster and save time. Essentially, we want to automate the manual repetitive boring tasks.



Mostly, we build these automations using N8N and Claude. And right now we are looking for a person who can help us to do that.



You do NOT need to be a senior expert for this role.

We’re open to junior-level freelancers as long as you have hands-on experience with N8N and a good understanding of how automations work.



This is not a full-time position, we’re looking for a freelancer we can reach out to whenever we need new automations.



Who This Role Is Perfect For:

-> You have experience building automations using N8N

-> You’ve worked with APIs, webhooks, or custom functions (JavaScript/Python)

-> You can follow simple briefs and build clean, logical flows

-> You’re reliable, respond quickly, and don’t disappear mid-task



What You’ll Do:

-> Build workflows (N8N or sometimes outside of it) that automate manual repetitive tasks (like searching for video ideas, writing scripts for videos, etc…)

-> Help us to save time with automations, with the goal of automating/removing the manual work as much as it’s possible

-> Keep things simple and efficient, no overengineering



Pay & Perks:

-> Task-based payments, based on complexity

-> Fully remote: work from anywhere

-> No meetings or handholding, we send you a task, you deliver the project

-> Ongoing freelance work (weekly or monthly tasks as needed)

-> Opportunity for long-term collaboration as we scale



How to Apply:

Fill out this short application form:




If you know how to build clean N8N automations and want creative flexible work with a team that values speed and simplicity, we’d love to hear from you.



Let’s build better systems together.

title: Styling and decor admin assistant
description:
Bali Brides | Bali Based Wedding Planning Company 



About Us

We’re a fast-growing Bali-based wedding company managing 80–100 weddings per year, with plans to scale to 150+ by 2027.

This is a brand new role designed to support our planning team across the creative and admin side of wedding styling. We’re not just filling a gap, we’re building a team for the long term.



?? Read This First-This Role Isn’t for Everyone

This role is for self-starters only.

Your onboarding will be self-guided through training modules and Loom walkthroughs over your first 4 weeks. After that, you’ll be expected to manage your workload independently.

If you need daily check-ins, constant direction, or someone to manage your time, this is not the right fit.

If you thrive on autonomy, ownership, and being trusted to get things done, keep reading.





What You’ll Do

Create mood boards and styling concepts from planner briefs (Canva/ google presentations or similar tools)

Liaise with florists, furniture, lighting and décor suppliers to source and compare quotes

Translate client vision into clear, actionable supplier briefs

Coordinate stationery selections, proofing, and approvals with our in-house team and clients

Keep styling documents updated and support the run sheet team as event day approaches





Who We’re Looking For

Experience in wedding styling, event coordination, or admin-heavy roles

Strong written and verbal English (client-facing communication is key)

Highly organised with strong attention to detail and aesthetics

Able to manage multiple weddings at different stages simultaneously

Self-directed, reliable, and proactive





Bonus skills:



Canva / Google Slides / Photoshop

AI image tools or mood board creation

Strong visual eye for styling and asthetics 





The Role

Full-time from day one 9am-5pm Monday - Friday - Flexible 

3-month paid training and onboarding probation period

Remote optional, with fortnightly planner check-ins (counted as work hours)





During off-season (Nov–Feb), the role shifts into:

Lookbook creation

Trend research

Marketing support

Managing our Etsy store 





Salary & Growth

USD $600-$1000 month, negotiable depending on experience, skill level, and speed

We are a fast-moving company.

 We value efficiency and output, we’d rather hire one exceptional, fast operator than multiple slow ones. You’ll be rewarded accordingly.





Perks

Potential to come to Bali once a year (after completion of 12 months) to see weddings come to life - (Not to work but to observe and have a break / meet the team)

Joining the team on occasional oversees trips for those who exceed thier KPIS

A genuinely close, supportive team environment

???? We work hard and we have fun harder.







Final Note (Important)

This is an admin-heavy role with a creative edge- not a “fun, easy wedding job.”

You will be expected to:

Think independently

Manage your own workload

Deliver consistently without being micromanaged



???? If this sounds like you:

We’d love to hear from you.

Please send your CV, A cover letter with position applying for and a 60 second introduction video telling us why you would be the best fit for this role.

title: Funnel Builder (Funnelish)
description:
We are an e-commerce/dropshipping company looking for a talented funnel editor. Funnels are the backbone of our business, and we’re looking for a detail-oriented Funnel Editor to join us.



Role Overview

You will be responsible for:

-Copying/cloning competitor funnels and/or building new funnels in Funnelish.

-Translating & adapting funnels into different languages using AI.

-Building new funnels based on templates & AI prompts.

-Making sure funnels look clean, on-brand, and functional across devices.

-Following SOPs to complete tasks quickly and accurately.



Requirements

-Experience with Funnelish 

-Strong eye for design and attention to detail (clean layouts, spacing, alignment).

-Comfortable using AI tools (ChatGPT, translation prompts, etc.) to speed up work and increase quality.

-Knowledge of copywriting principles.

-Can follow instructions but mainly think independently and deliver fast turnaround.

-Reliable internet connection & good communication in English.



Nice to Have (not required):

-Basic understanding of conversion optimization (headlines, CTAs, layouts).

-Basic understanding of copywriting principles

-Familiarity with Shopify (as backend) is a plus.



What We Offer

-The opportunity to be part of a growing business. We have big plans!

-Long-term role 

-We will provide SOPs (WIP), training, and AI prompts

-Opportunity to grow with us as we scale 



How to Apply

-Send us a short loom intro about yourself 

-Share examples/screenshots of funnels you’ve built or cloned.

-Add “Funnel Builder” in your subject line so we know you read the full post.

title: UI/UX Mobile Designer
description:
UI/UX Mobile Designer



Job Overview

We are looking for a talented and detail-oriented UI/UX Mobile Designer to lead the design and user experience of our mobile application. This role will play a key part in shaping intuitive, user-centered designs throughout various stages of the product lifecycle.



Key Responsibilities

* Design engaging, user-friendly mobile interfaces with a strong focus on usability and aesthetics

* Oversee the overall design and user experience of the app across different product cycles, from concept to iteration

* Collaborate closely with software engineers and the Product Lead to create effective and testable screen designs

* Develop wireframes, prototypes, and high-fidelity UI designs for mobile applications

* Participate in and support A/B testing by designing alternative user flows and screen variations

* Ensure consistency in design systems, patterns, and branding across the app

* Stay updated with the latest UI/UX trends, especially within iOS design guidelines

* Redesign layouts for better usability and clarity

* Work alongside the Product Lead and engineering team to align design with business and technical goals

* Provide guidance and train the current Product Lead in UI/UX best practices and principles



Qualifications

* Minimum of 2+ years of experience in UI/UX design

* Strong portfolio demonstrating mobile app design work

* Proficiency in design tools such as Figma, Sketch, Adobe XD, or similar

* Solid understanding of usability principles, user flows, and interaction design

* Experience working in collaborative, cross-functional teams

* Familiarity with design systems and component libraries



How to apply:

Please apply ONLY through this Google Form.

Do not send messages on onlinejobs.ph.



Application form link:

title: Video Editor (VSL + Short-Form Ads)
description:
Contract | Remote | $25-30 per video | Growth + Bonus Opportunities



We’re looking for a skilled and reliable video editor to join our remote creative team and help us produce high-performing direct-response VSLs that blend storytelling, smart pacing, and scroll-stopping visuals.



These are 1–2-minute videos combining AI voiceovers, real-world footage, and TikTok-style B-roll to deliver emotional, authentic stories that convert.



You’ll be provided with everything you need — script, voiceover, direction, and examples — your job is to bring it to life through pacing, visuals, and clean editing.



???? What You’ll Be Doing



Editing up to 6 VSLs per week (bonus pay available for extra volume)



Using CapCut for editing (or similar tools)



Matching visuals, pacing, and music to scripted AI voiceovers



Pulling B-roll from Canva Pro, stock footage, or TikTok-style clips



Delivering emotional, polished, and conversion-focused edits



???? What We Provide



Fully written scripts + recorded voiceovers



Brand assets and creative direction



Reference videos + editing tutorials



Organized folders for each project



Friendly, responsive feedback — we love editors who want to grow



???? Pay & Bonuses



$25-30 per video, depending on skill and experience



Bonus pay for taking on additional projects (up to 15 total)



Room to grow into long-term creative roles (direction, scripting, ideation, etc.)



???? What We’re Looking For



Strong skills in CapCut, Canva Pro, and TikTok-style editing



Fast turnaround and clean file delivery



Attention to detail — syncing visuals to emotion and script



Marketing instincts — you understand story, pacing, and engagement



Ability to follow brand direction and take feedback well



Bonus: experience editing UGC, eCommerce, or VSLs



???? Why Work With Us



We’re a fast-moving team building digital brands rooted in storytelling, quality, and performance. We value creativity, communication, and consistency — and we reward editors who bring passion and precision to their work.

We believe in fair pay, clear expectations, and long-term growth for people who deliver and want to keep improving.



If you want to join a small but driven team where your creative input truly matters, we’d love to hear from you.



???? To Apply



Please send:



A short intro message



Your portfolio (VSLs, TikTok-style edits, UGC welcome)



Your rate per video



How many videos per week you’re comfortable starting with



Looking forward to working with you.

title: Accounts Clerk (Xero)
description:
We are seeking an Accounts Clerk to assist with the day-to-day finance operations across our jewellery brands. This role is responsible for maintaining accurate financial records, handling routine accounting tasks, and supporting both accounts payable and accounts receivable processes. The right candidate will be organised, detail-focused, and able to manage their workload efficiently while meeting deadlines.



Responsibilities:

Entering financial data accurately for all brands

Creating bills and invoices and ensuring correct coding to accounts

Processing and scheduling payments through the bank

Maintaining accurate records with scanned documentation linked to invoices and purchases

Reconciling bank accounts, credit cards, and payment platforms on a regular basis

Supporting accounts receivable and accounts payable processes

Assisting with payroll preparation and superannuation

Assisting with reconciliation of stock and suspense accounts

Generating purchase orders and liaising with suppliers regarding timing and payments

Supporting import invoice processing and carrier/shipping account payments

Assisting with end-of-month reporting

Liaising with customers and suppliers regarding payments and related queries



Requirements:

At least 2–3 years of experience in an accounts clerk or similar finance role

Degree or certification in accounting, finance, or related field preferred

Proficiency in Xero (training or certification highly regarded)

Familiarity with eCommerce and retail accounting practices (experience with jewellery industry highly regarded)

Strong Excel skills, including use of CSV/XLS files and advanced functions

Highly organised and detail-focused

Excellent communication skills with proficiency in English

Ability to work under pressure and meet deadlines

Capable of working autonomously while raising issues proactively when identified



Software to Be Used:

Xero

Microsoft Excel / Google Sheets



Timezone: AEST



Working Hours: 2 hours per day / 10 hours per week

title: Graphic Designer
description:
We are looking for a talented Senior Digital Designer who can produce high-quality visual assets for marketing campaigns, website graphics, and UI components. Experience in Gaming, online entertainment, or high-conversion marketing design is highly preferred.



Key Responsibilities

Marketing & Advertising Design

• Design promotional banners, campaign creatives, and landing page visuals

• Produce social media graphics for platforms such as
, Instagram,
, and other marketing channels

• Create advertising creatives for digital campaigns (Meta Ads, Google Ads, etc.)

• Design promotional graphics for events, and seasonal campaigns

Website & UI Design

• Create UI components and visual assets for gaming platform interfaces

• Design icons, thumbnails, and game promotional graphics

• Assist with improving visual layout and user experience on web and mobile platforms

Brand & Visual Identity

• Maintain a consistent visual style across marketing channels and platforms

• Work with marketing and product teams to design engaging user-focused visuals

AI-Assisted Creative Production

• Use AI image generation tools to accelerate creative production

• Generate and refine design assets using tools such as Stable Diffusion, OpenArt, Midjourney, or similar

• Combine AI-generated visuals with manual design work to produce marketing materials and UI assets



Requirements

• 3+ years experience in digital graphic design

• Strong skills in Adobe Photoshop, Illustrator, or similar tools

• Experience designing marketing creatives and digital advertising graphics

• Understanding of mobile-first design and responsive layouts

• Ability to work independently in a remote environment

• Good English communication skills



Preferred Experience

title: E-commerce Supply Chain Manager (Shopify / Fulfillment / 3PL)
description:
IMPORTANT: Only apply if you have real experience managing supply chain and fulfillment for DTC e-commerce brands (physical products). If you do not have this experience, do NOT apply.



We are looking for a Supply Chain Manager to take ownership of our fulfillment and supplier operations.



This is not a basic VA role. You will be responsible for making sure orders are delivered on time, problems are prevented, and systems are built to scale.



Your responsibilities:



* Manage supplier relationships and communication

* Ensure orders are fulfilled on time and correctly

* Monitor shipping times, delays, and performance

* Identify and solve fulfillment issues before they become problems

* Build systems and processes for tracking orders and performance

* Work with 3PLs and fulfillment partners

* Improve delivery times, reliability, and customer experience



This role is for someone who:



* Takes ownership of operations

* Thinks in systems, not tasks

* Is proactive (not reactive)

* Can handle pressure and solve problems fast



Requirements:



* Proven experience with DTC e-commerce brands (must)

* Experience managing suppliers and fulfillment

* Experience with Shopify order flow

* Strong understanding of logistics and shipping processes

* Ability to analyze and improve systems

* Fluent English



We are NOT looking for:



* Basic virtual assistants

* Customer support agents

* People without real supply chain experience



To apply, you MUST answer:



1. How many years of experience do you have in e-commerce supply chain?



2. What kind of brands have you worked with?

   (Be specific — DTC, physical products?)



3. What suppliers or fulfillment partners have you worked with?



4. How would you setup a supply chain management system where you have complete overview of all orders with problems and delivery?



5. How do you track and improve delivery performance?



6. Record a short Loom (max 2 minutes) explaining your experience.



Applications that do not answer ALL questions will not be reviewed.



We are looking for someone long-term who can build and own this part of the business.

These are 1–2-minute videos combining AI voiceovers, real-world footage, and TikTok-style B-roll to deliver emotional, authentic stories that convert.



You’ll be provided with everything you need — script, voiceover, direction, and examples — your job is to bring it to life through pacing, visuals, and clean editing.

Editing up to 6 VSLs per week (bonus pay available for extra volume)



Using CapCut for editing (or similar tools)



Matching visuals, pacing, and music to scripted AI voiceovers

Delivering emotional, polished, and conversion-focused edits

Fully written scripts + recorded voiceovers



Brand assets and creative direction



Reference videos + editing tutorials



Organized folders for each project



Friendly, responsive feedback — we love editors who want to grow



???? Pay & Bonuses



$30-35 per video, depending on skill and experience



Bonus pay for taking on additional projects (up to 15 total)



Room to grow into long-term creative roles (direction, scripting, ideation, etc.)



???? What We’re Looking For



Strong skills in CapCut, Canva Pro, and TikTok-style editing



Fast turnaround and clean file delivery



Attention to detail — syncing visuals to emotion and script



Marketing instincts — you understand story, pacing, and engagement



Ability to follow brand direction and take feedback well



Bonus: experience editing UGC, eCommerce, or VSLs



???? Why Work With Us



We’re a fast-moving team building digital brands rooted in storytelling, quality, and performance. We value creativity, communication, and consistency — and we reward editors who bring passion and precision to their work.

We believe in fair pay, clear expectations, and long-term growth for people who deliver and want to keep improving.

Looking forward to working with you.

title: Part-Time Digital Marketing Specialist (Google Ads + Meta Ads)
description:
About Us

We run two distinct product lines from Australia. The first is an education and personal development brand for young entrepreneurs. The second is a collection of published books (fiction and non-fiction). Both sell direct to consumer through Shopify and Woocommerce, with digital delivery via our own platforms.



We are looking for someone who lives and breathes paid digital marketing. Someone who can look at a campaign, spot what is underperforming, and fix it. Someone who gets excited about lowering cost per acquisition and finding new angles that convert.



What This Role Is

This is a marketing role. Your primary focus will be planning, building, and optimising paid ad campaigns across Meta 
 and Instagram) and Google Ads. You will also support organic social content, but the core of this role is performance marketing.



You will be responsible for:

- setting up google and google analytics accounts

- Building and managing Meta ad campaigns (awareness, traffic, conversion)

- Building and managing Google Ads campaigns (search, display, shopping where relevant)

- Monitoring and reporting on key metrics: CAC, ROAS, CTR, CPC, conversion rates

- Testing creative angles, audiences, and copy variations

- Recommending budget allocation based on performance data

- Creating simple ad creative (static images, carousel layouts, short copy) using Figma

- Supporting social media content as a secondary responsibility (scheduling, community replies, simple posts)



What This Role Is Not

This is not a general virtual assistant position. We are not looking for someone to manage inboxes, book travel, or handle admin tasks. If your experience is primarily in general VA work, this role will not be the right fit.



About You

- You have hands-on experience running Meta Ads and Google Ads campaigns, with real results to show for it

- You understand customer acquisition cost, lifetime value, and how to optimise spend against these metrics

- You can set up tracking (Meta Pixel, Google Tag Manager, conversion events) and troubleshoot when data looks wrong

- You are comfortable working independently, making recommendations, and flagging issues early

- You have a portfolio or case studies showing campaigns you have managed and the results you achieved



Portfolio is required. Please include screenshots, reports, or a document showing campaigns you have worked on, the strategy behind them, and measurable outcomes (ROAS, CAC reduction, conversion improvements, audience growth). Applications without a portfolio will not be considered.



Nice to Have

- Experience with Shopify and Woocommerce stores or direct-to-consumer brands

- Familiarity with
 marketing tools (Klaviyo, Mailchimp, or similar)

- Basic video editing for short-form ad creative (Reels, Stories)

- Experience marketing books, courses, or education products



The Details

- Part-time (approximately 15 hours per week to start, with room to grow)

- Long-term engagement preferred



You will work directly with the founder

Compensation discussed during interview, based on experience



How to Apply

Send a short message introducing yourself. Include:

- A link to your portfolio or attached case studies showing real campaign results

- Which platforms you have the most experience with (Meta, Google, or both)

- One example of a campaign you improved and what you changed to get better results



We read every application. If your experience aligns, we will be in touch quickly.

title: ### GoHighLevel Funnels and Automations Expert | Immediate Start | Remote ###
description:

We’re a Real Estate Wholesale company hiring a GoHighLevel expert to manage our GoHighLevel platform in 2 areas: 



1. Building webpages and funnels using the GoHighLevel Builder

2. Building and managing automations that handle all communications with Sellers and Buyers/Investors.



This is a hands-on role. You will build, run, and optimize funnels and multi-channel follow-up systems that nurture leads and increase appointments and contracts.



We need someone who’s FAST, RELIABLE, PERFORMANCE-DRIVEN, & HONEST with their time.



What you’ll do

You will build and manage landing pages, websites, and funnels using GoHighLevel Builder.

You will create and optimize forms, calendars, surveys, and lead capture flows inside GoHighLevel.

You will manage all communications for Sellers and Buyers/Investors through automated sequences.

You will build GoHighLevel Workflows including triggers, branching, stop rules, tagging, and routing.

You will build AI-assisted workflows using GPT-style tools to personalize messages, summarize conversations, detect intent, and route next steps.

You will manage multi-channel sequences including
, SMS, RVM (ringless voi
), and AI Voice follow-up.

You will own

You will track performance weekly and improve results through testing and iteration.

You will maintain CRM hygiene including pipeline stages, tags, custom fields, tasks, and accurate logging of all touchpoints.



Requirements (must-have)

3+ years experience in GoHighLevel funnels, marketing automation, lifecycle communications, or CR
ssaging with hands-on execution.

Strong GoHighLevel experience including funnel/page building and workflow automation.

Strong AI + automation experience including prompt workflows, GPT personalization, and automation logic.

Experience with integrations including Zapier/Make, webhooks, and connecting external tools to GoHighLevel.

Performance-driven mindset with comfort owning KPIs and improving them through testing.

Real estate experience is a big advantage and CRM experience is required.





Nice to have

Experience with GA4, GTM, Meta/Google pixels and basic attribution.



How to apply
